description.es: TanStack Router 1.170.4 estrena CSS runtime inline en Start, Next.js 16.3 entra en canary y shadcn CLI 4.7 soporta package.json#imports y target aliases.
description.en: TanStack Router 1.170.4 lands inline CSS runtime controls in Start, Next.js 16.3 enters canary, and shadcn CLI 4.7 ships package.json#imports plus target aliases.
description.fr: TanStack Router 1.170.4 introduit le runtime CSS inline dans Start, Next.js 16.3 entre en canary et shadcn CLI 4.7 livre package.json#imports et les target aliases.

title.es: TanStack Router 1.170.4 + Start 1.168.6: CSS runtime inline y URL templates
title.en: TanStack Router 1.170.4 + Start 1.168.6: inline CSS runtime and asset URL templates
title.fr: TanStack Router 1.170.4 + Start 1.168.6 : runtime CSS inline et URL templates

body_html.es: El release del 17 may bumpea @tanstack/react-router a 1.170.4, @tanstack/react-start a 1.168.6 y router-core a 1.171.2. Llegan inline CSS runtime controls y asset URL templates en Start, más un fix para paths con guion bajo literal bajo layouts sin path. Ojo si usás Start: ahora podés ajustar cómo se inyecta el CSS en runtime sin tocar el bundler, y los assets son referenciables por template — clave para builds multi-tenant.
body_html.en: The May 17 release bumps @tanstack/react-router to 1.170.4, @tanstack/react-start to 1.168.6 and router-core to 1.171.2. Start gains inline CSS runtime controls and asset URL templates, plus a fix for literal underscore paths under pathless layouts. If you ship on Start, you can now tune runtime CSS injection without poking the bundler, and assets are template-addressable — clutch for multi-tenant builds.
body_html.fr: La release du 17 mai monte @tanstack/react-router à 1.170.4, @tanstack/react-start à 1.168.6 et router-core à 1.171.2. Start gagne les inline CSS runtime controls et les asset URL templates, plus un fix pour les paths avec underscore littéral sous layouts sans path. Côté Start, vous ajustez désormais l'injection CSS runtime sans toucher le bundler.

title.es: Next.js 16.3.0-canary.7 abre el ciclo del próximo minor
title.en: Next.js 16.3.0-canary.7 opens the next minor cycle
title.fr: Next.js 16.3.0-canary.7 ouvre le cycle du prochain minor

body_html.es: Vercel publicó next@16.3.0-canary.7 en npm el 15 may, arrancando la rama 16.3 después del parche de seguridad 16.2.6 de la semana pasada. Todavía no hay blog post de features estables — canary sirve para probar Turbopack y RSC fixes antes del estable. Si self-hosteás Next.js, seguí en 16.2.6 (o 15.5.18) hasta que salga 16.3 GA; el canary es para validar la migración temprano.
body_html.en: Vercel published next@16.3.0-canary.7 to npm on May 15, kicking off the 16.3 line after last week's 16.2.6 security patch. There's no stable feature blog yet — canary is the channel for vetting Turbopack and RSC fixes before they land in stable. If you self-host Next, stay on 16.2.6 (or 15.5.18) until 16.3 GA; the canary is for early migration testing.
body_html.fr: Vercel a publié next@16.3.0-canary.7 sur npm le 15 mai, ouvrant la branche 16.3 après le patch de sécurité 16.2.6 de la semaine passée. Pas encore de blog stable — canary sert à valider Turbopack et les fixes RSC avant la GA. Si vous self-hostez, restez sur 16.2.6 (ou 15.5.18) jusqu'à la 16.3 GA.

title.es: shadcn CLI 4.7 soporta package.json#imports y target aliases
title.en: shadcn CLI 4.7 ships package.json#imports and target aliases
title.fr: shadcn CLI 4.7 prend en charge package.json#imports et les target aliases

body_html.es: El nuevo shadcn@4.7.0 ya entiende package.json#imports para instalar, reescribir imports y resolver registries de terceros. En vez de depender solo de compilerOptions.paths de TS, podés usar aliases privados estilo #components/* y referenciarlos desde components.json. Los registry items además aceptan target aliases en files[].target (ej. "target": "@ui/ai/prompt-input.tsx") — un alivio gigante en monorepos donde shared UI vive en un package y app code en otro.
body_html.en: The new shadcn@4.7.0 reads package.json#imports for installing components, rewriting imports, and resolving third-party registries. Instead of leaning only on TS compilerOptions.paths, you can use private aliases like #components/* and reference them from components.json. Registry items can also use target aliases in files[].target (e.g. "target": "@ui/ai/prompt-input.tsx") — a huge ergonomics win in monorepos where shared UI lives in one package and app code in another.
body_html.fr: shadcn@4.7.0 lit désormais package.json#imports pour installer, réécrire les imports et résoudre les registries tiers. À la place de compilerOptions.paths, vous utilisez des aliases privés type #components/*. Les registry items acceptent aussi des target aliases dans files[].target — énorme gain en monorepo.

body_html.es: El release publicó 22 paquetes en un solo tag: react-router@1.170.4, solid-router@1.170.4, vue-router@1.170.4, react-start@1.168.6, router-core@1.171.2, plus zod-adapter, devtools y eslint-plugin actualizados. Manuel Schiller lideró el PR de inline CSS runtime y el fix de underscore paths. Si tenés monorepo con TanStack, bumpealo en bloque para evitar version skew entre router y adapters.
body_html.en: The release pushed 22 packages under one tag: react-router@1.170.4, solid-router@1.170.4, vue-router@1.170.4, react-start@1.168.6, router-core@1.171.2, plus updated zod-adapter, devtools, and eslint-plugin. Manuel Schiller led both the inline CSS runtime PR and the underscore-path fix. If you ship a TanStack-heavy monorepo, bump the whole set together to avoid version skew between router and adapters.
body_html.fr: 22 paquets publiés sous un seul tag, dont react-router@1.170.4, solid-router@1.170.4, vue-router@1.170.4, react-start@1.168.6, router-core@1.171.2. Bumpez l'ensemble pour éviter le skew router/adapters.

title.es: Chrome stable: refresh desktop + Android el 12 may
title.en: Chrome stable: desktop + Android refresh on May 12
title.fr: Chrome stable : refresh desktop + Android le 12 mai

body_html.es: El equipo de Chrome publicó updates de Stable Channel for Desktop y Stable Update for Android el martes 12 may, más una tanda de beta (desktop, Android, iOS, ChromeOS) y dev al día siguiente. Si seguís Chrome 145 en producción, revisá la entrada de Extended Stable que también recibió bumps — son fixes y patches de seguridad típicos de mid-cycle, sin features nuevas notables.
body_html.en: The Chrome team published a Stable Channel for Desktop and Stable Update for Android on Tuesday May 12, followed by a Beta wave (desktop, Android, iOS, ChromeOS) and a Dev refresh the next day. If you track Chrome 145 in prod, check the Extended Stable entry — bumps and security patches, typical mid-cycle housekeeping, no shiny new features.
body_html.fr: Chrome a publié des updates Stable Desktop et Stable Android le 12 mai, suivis d'une vague Beta et Dev. Patches de sécurité mid-cycle, pas de nouveautés notables.

notable_trends.es: Semana tranquila después del rewrite Rust de Bun y el patch de seguridad masivo de Next.js: el ecosistema está en modo housekeeping. Si venías postergando bumpear monorepos con TanStack o shadcn, esta es la ventana — los releases son aditivos y los riesgos están bien acotados.
notable_trends.en: Quiet week following Bun's Rust rewrite and Next.js's mass security patch — the ecosystem is in housekeeping mode. If you've been postponing TanStack or shadcn monorepo bumps, this is the window. Releases are additive and risk surface is small.
notable_trends.fr: Semaine calme après la réécriture Rust de Bun et le patch de sécurité massif de Next.js : l'écosystème est en mode housekeeping. Si vous repoussiez les bumps TanStack ou shadcn, c'est la fenêtre — releases additifs, risque maîtrisé.
